title: dianthus sylvestris subsp. longibracteatus
intro: Dianthus sylvestris subsp. longibracteatus, also known as the Long-Bracted Fringed Pink, is a subspecies of the Dianthus sylvestris plant, which is a flowering plant native to Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The Long-Bracted Fringed Pink is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Herbal preparations of Dianthus sylvestris subsp. longibracteatus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
